action.reasoning: Observation: Iter_13 severely regressed to 12.4% success (down from best 49.6% in iter_8). Analysis: The oversampled large-network setup diverged, destroying performance on previously solved tasks. With only ~1 hour remaining, the safest way to improve the 49.6% best score is to continue training from the stable iter_8 checkpoint (4-frame stack, 512-dim features, [512,256] MLP, 15M steps) rather than risk another unstable config. Adding ~5M more steps should take ~40 minutes, fitting in the remaining budget. Decision: Update train.py to support loading iter_8 via --load_path and continuing PPO training with identical 4-frame stack wrappers. Update predict.py to read a saved config.json so it uses the correct frame_stack for the new checkpoint. Launch 5M-step continuation and then evaluate.
